title: MachineParts Object
description: Object that represents a collection of machine parts. These parts are the children of another part or the collection of base parts from MachineKinematics.

methods.name: add
methods.description: Add a new part to this collection. The part's parent will be set to the owner of this collection, or null if this is the root parts collection.   If the passed MachinePartInput has a MachineAxisInput a new MachineAxis will be created.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: item
methods.description: Get the part at index in this collection.

methods.name: itemById
methods.description: Get the part with the given ID.

properties.name: count
properties.description: Get the number of parts in this collection.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

add.title: MachineParts.add Method
add.description: Add a new part to this collection. The part's parent will be set to the owner of this collection, or null if this is the root parts collection. If the passed MachinePartInput has a MachineAxisInput a new MachineAxis will be created.
add.return_description: Returns the newly created MachinePart or null if creation failed.

parameters.name: partInput
parameters.description: Part input object used to create the new MachinePart.

classType.title: MachineParts.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

createPartInput.title: MachineParts.createPartInput Method
createPartInput.return_description: Returns the new MachinePartInput or null if creation failed.

parameters.name: partType
parameters.description: The type of part to create. When this parameter is Axis, you must set a value for axisInput.

item.title: MachineParts.item Method
item.description: Get the part at index in this collection.

itemById.title: MachineParts.itemById Method
itemById.description: Get the part with the given ID.
itemById.return_description: Returns the MachinePart with the given ID, or null if the given ID does not match any part in the collection.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID for the part to get.

count.title: MachineParts.count Property
count.description: Get the number of parts in this collection.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = machineParts_var.count

isValid.title: MachineParts.isValid Property
isValid.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = machineParts_var.isValid

objectType.title: MachineParts.objectType Property
objectType.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object. It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = machineParts_var.objectType
